steps.title: Distribute the monomial
steps.content: Multiply $x^{-9}y^6z^5$ by each term inside the parentheses: $$(x^{-9}y^6z^5)(x^4z^5)+(x^{-9}y^6z^5)(y^8z^{-7}).$$

steps.title: Add exponents on matching bases
steps.content: For the first product, $$x^{-9}x^4y^6z^5z^5=x^{-5}y^6z^{10}.$$ For the second product, $$x^{-9}y^6y^8z^5z^{-7}=x^{-9}y^{14}z^{-2}.$$

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: The product is $$x^{-5}y^6z^{10}+x^{-9}y^{14}z^{-2}.$$ This matches choice B.

steps.title: Why the exponents combine this way
steps.content: When multiplying powers with the same base, add the exponents. That is why $x^{-9}x^4=x^{-5}$, $y^6y^8=y^{14}$, and $z^5z^{-7}=z^{-2}$.
